items.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder

children.content: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

gallery.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

thumbnail.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: After Little Baby's death, Canavan demands government stop 'running scared'

children.content: Nationals leader Matthew Canavan says after the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the government must "move on" from its defeat at the Voice referendum and re-engage on Indigenous policy debate.

image.altText: Nationals Leader Matt Canavan gesturing while wearing a suit, with a screen behind him.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's grandfather thanks community, says time to 'let justice take its course'

children.content: The family of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs on Thursday, has thanked the community for their support as tributes flow in from across the country.

image.altText: A teddy bear among floral tributes

items.title: Seeing Kakadu through the lens of Crocodile Dundee

children.content: Crew members recount filming scenes in some of Kakadu National Park's most iconic locations, forty years on from Crocodile Dundee's release.

gallery.altText: Man holds crocodile around the neck and smile sitting on bank of waterway

thumbnail.altText: Man holds crocodile around the neck and smile sitting on bank of waterway

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

image.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

items.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for man who killed her

children.content: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

image.altText: A family stands in front of the NT Supreme Court looking sad and wearing Justice for Lena shirts.

items.title: Chinese company launches legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin

children.content: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.

gallery.altText: A large piece of equipment on the port.

thumbnail.altText: A large piece of equipment on the port.

items.title: Tough-on-crime NT government snubs youth incarceration Senate inquiry

children.content: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: A group of young Indigenous children are playing basketball inside the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre.

items.title: NT paramedics 'beaten' to the point of 'semi-consciousness'

children.content: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

image.altText: An ambulance pulled side of road at dusk in city

items.title: Trump says resuming strikes in Iran 'a possibility'

children.content: The US president told reporters he is reviewing a new peace proposal from Iranian negotiators. Follow live.

image.altText: Donald Trump leans towards a group of reporters in front of the presidential plane Air Force One.

items.title: 'If the cops came, you'd slip out the back': The shifting role of Aussie pubs

children.content: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

image.altText: Graphic illustration of arms and hands holding schooners of beer 'cheersing' under old wrought iron balcony decoration

items.title: 'I made the choice': Why James Valentine resolved to die on his own terms

children.content: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

items.title: Beck spent decades as an alt-rock anomaly. Now he's going symphonic

items.title: Farmers say levees built to prevent flooding have made situation worse

children.content: In Western Australia's biggest food bowl region, farmers are still assessing the damage from the latest flood caused by Cyclone Narelle. However, farmers say the levees built to reduce damage made the situation worse.
